title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The tool itself poses minimal direct risk as it is designed to enhance security by identifying vulnerabilities in dependencies. However, if misused or if it contains flaws, it could expose sensitive information or lead to false positives/negatives, potentially impacting organizations relying on it.
recommended_actions: Evaluate the tool in a controlled environment before integrating it into production workflows.
Monitor for updates or patches to the tool itself, as it is open-source and may evolve rapidly.
Educate teams on proper usage and validate its findings with additional vulnerability scanning tools.

title: Net::IMAP: Denial of Service via incomplete raw argument validation
description:
### Summary

Several Net::IMAP commands accept a raw string argument which is only validated to prevent CRLF injection and then sent verbatim.  If this string is derived from user-controlled input, an attacker can force the next command to be absorbed as a continuation of the first command.  This will cause the first command to eventually fail, but also prevents it from returning until another command is sent (from another thread).  That other command will not return until the connection is clos

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity. CVE: CVE-2026-47241.

title: Net::IMAP: Command Injection via non-synchronizing literal in "raw" argument
description:
Several Net::IMAP commands accept a "raw data" argument that is sent verbatim after validation to prevent command injection.  However, if a server does not support non-synchronizing literals, it may still be possible to inject arbitrary IMAP commands inside non-synchronizing literals.

### Details

Raw data arguments support embedded literal values, both synchronizing and non-synchronizing.  Non-synchronizing literals can only be safely sent when the server advertises any of the `LITERAL+`, `LIT

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to inject arbitrary IMAP commands via non-synchronizing literals, potentially leading to unauthorized access, data exfiltration, or manipulation of email servers.
recommended_actions: Apply the latest security patches or updates to the affected Net::IMAP library to mitigate the vulnerability.
Ensure IMAP servers are configured to support non-synchronizing literals securely or disable their use if not required.
Monitor IMAP server logs for unusual or unauthorized command patterns that may indicate exploitation attempts.

SQL clause without parameterization or input validation. A remote, authenticated attacker holding the tokens/update permission on a survey can inject a crafted array element to perform SQL injection. title: Missing authentication for critical function in Microsoft PC Manager allows an authorized...
description: Missing authentication for critical function in Microsoft PC Manager allows an authorized attacker to elevate privileges locally.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in Microsoft PC Manager allows an attacker with local access to exploit missing authentication mechanisms, potentially leading to privilege escalation and unauthorized control over the system. This could result in data theft, system compromise, or further exploitation of the affected machine.
recommended_actions: Apply the latest security patch or update for Microsoft PC Manager as soon as it becomes available.
Restrict local access to trusted personnel and implement strict access control policies.
Monitor systems for unusual activity and review logs for any signs of exploitation.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-48293.

title: LimeSurvey constructs account password-reset links from the client-supplied HTTP Host header...
description: LimeSurvey constructs account password-reset links from the client-supplied HTTP Host header without validating it. The optional allowedHosts allowlist that would constrain this is undefined in the default (and documented) configuration, so LSHttpRequest::checkIsAllowedHost() results in no operation. A remote, unauthenticated attacker who submits a forgotten-password request for a known account (requiring only the target's username and email) with a spoofed Host header causes LimeSurvey to email

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: This vulnerability allows a remote, unauthenticated attacker to exploit the password-reset functionality by manipulating the Host header, potentially leading to account compromise. If exploited, attackers could gain unauthorized access to user accounts, steal sensitive information, or escalate privileges, depending on the permissions of the compromised accounts.
recommended_actions: Immediately configure the 'allowedHosts' allowlist in LimeSurvey to restrict valid Host headers to trusted domains.
Apply any available patches or updates from LimeSurvey addressing CVE-2026-50635.
Monitor account activity for unusual password reset requests and notify users of potential phishing attempts.

title: Dell Inventory Collector Client, versions prior to 13.8.0, contain an Improper Link Resolution...
description: Dell Inventory Collector Client, versions prior to 13.8.0, contain an Improper Link Resolution Before File Access ('Link Following') vulnerability. A low privileged attacker with local access could potentially exploit this vulnerability, leading to Arbitrary File Write.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows a low-privileged attacker with local access to exploit an improper link resolution issue, potentially leading to arbitrary file writes. This could result in privilege escalation, data corruption, or system compromise.
recommended_actions: Upgrade Dell Inventory Collector Client to version 13.8.0 or later to mitigate the vulnerability.
Restrict local access to systems running vulnerable versions to trusted personnel only.
Monitor systems for unusual file modifications or access patterns that may indicate exploitation attempts.

title: CVE-2026-10566 - A weakness has been identified in FoundationAgents MetaGPT up to 0.8.2. This affects the function Me
description: A weakness has been identified in FoundationAgents MetaGPT up to 0.8.2. This affects the function Message.check_instruct_content of the file metagpt/schema.py. Executing a manipulation of the argument mapping can lead to deserialization. The attack is restricted to local execution. The exploit has been made available to the public and could be used for attacks. The project was informed of the problem early through an issue report but has not responded yet.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-10566.

title: CVE-2026-10565 - A security flaw has been discovered in Open5GS up to 2.7.6. The impacted element is the function gmm
description: A security flaw has been discovered in Open5GS up to 2.7.6. The impacted element is the function gmm_state_security_mode of the file src/amf/gmm-sm.c of the component NGAP Handover. Performing a manipulation results in race condition. The attack can be initiated remotely. The complexity of an attack is rather high. The exploitability is regarded as difficult. The exploit has been released to the public and may be used for attacks. The pull request to fix this issue awaits acceptance.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in Open5GS could allow attackers to exploit a race condition in the NGAP Handover component, potentially disrupting mobile network operations or compromising sensitive data. However, the attack complexity is high, and exploitability is difficult, reducing the likelihood of widespread exploitation.
recommended_actions: Upgrade Open5GS to the latest version once the patch is released and accepted.
Monitor network traffic for unusual activity related to NGAP Handover and implement additional logging.
Engage with Open5GS maintainers to track the status of the pull request and expedite patch deployment.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-10510.

title: CVE-2026-10100 - The Simple Custom Login Page plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via t
description: The Simple Custom Login Page plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the color settings fields (Page Background, Form Background, Text Color, Link Color) in versions up to and including 1.0.3. This is due to insufficient input sanitization of the color option values (they were registered with register_setting() and stored via the Settings API/update_option() with no sanitize_callback) combined with the values being output into a <style> block on wp-login.php using esc_attr(), which is incorrect for a CSS context (it does not escape ;, {, }, / or *). This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with administrator-level access and above, to inject arbitrary CSS rules into the login page that are rendered for all unauthenticated visitors, enabling UI-redress and credential-phishing attacks.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-10100.

description: A security flaw has been discovered in NousResearch hermes-agent up to 2026.4.23. This affects the function _sync_anthropic_entry_from_credentials_file of the file agent/credential_pool.py of the component Credential Pool Synchronization. description: A weakness has been identified in westboy CicadasCMS up to 2431154dac8d0735e04f1fd2a3c3556668fc8dab. Impacted is an unknown function of the file src/main/java/com/zhiliao/module/web/system/ScheduleJobController.java of the component Task Scheduling Management Module. Executing a manipulation can lead to cross site scripting. The attack can be executed remotely. The exploit has been made available to the public and could be used for attacks. This product implements a rolling release for ongoing delivery, which means version information for affected or updated releases is unavailable. The project was informed of the problem early through an issue report but has not responded yet.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity. CVE: CVE-2026-10529.

title: CVE-2026-9050 - The Slider Revolution plugin for WordPress in versions 6.0.0-6.7.55 and 7.0.0-7.0.14 is vulnerable t
description: The Slider Revolution plugin for WordPress in versions 6.0.0-6.7.55 and 7.0.0-7.0.14 is vulnerable to unauthorized modification of data. This is due to the plugin not properly verifying that a user is authorized to perform an action. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with Contributor-level access and above, to deactivate any active plugin installed on the site.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-9050.

title: CVE-2026-9048 - The Slider Revolution plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Sensitive Information Exposure in versio
description: The Slider Revolution plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Sensitive Information Exposure in versions 7.0.0 - 7.0.14, via the 'slider.get.full' AJAX Action. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with Contributor-level access and above, to extract sensitive data including raw social media API credentials: the Instagram OAuth token, Flickr API key, YouTube Data API key, and Facebook App ID, stored in any configured slider's settings.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-9048.

title: CVE-2026-10528 - A security flaw has been discovered in Orthanc DICOM Server up to 1.12.11. This issue affects the fu
description: A security flaw has been discovered in Orthanc DICOM Server up to 1.12.11. This issue affects the function DcmItem::read of the file OrthancFramework/Sources/DicomParsing/FromDcmtkBridge.cpp of the component DCMTK Parser. Performing a manipulation results in stack-based buffer overflow. Attacking locally is a requirement. The exploit has been released to the public and may be used for attacks. The patch is named bae99026ca97. To fix this issue, it is recommended to deploy a patch.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity. CVE: CVE-2026-10528.
